title: Granadilla normalis
taxonomy: Granadilla normalis is also known as the passion fruit, a type of flowering vine native to South America. It belongs to the Passifloraceae family. The species Passiflora edulis is commonly referred to as Granadilla normalis. This classification is primarily based on its botanical characteristics and classification in the taxonomy of plants. The name 'Passiflora' comes from the Latin words 'passio' and 'flos,' meaning 'suffering' and 'flower,' respectively.
